title: BlackBerry Earnings Preview: Here's What to Watch for
text: BlackBerry was set to report Q1 earnings on June 24, 2021 after market close. The stock had gained approximately 128% over the preceding year and was trading near C$16.00. Wall Street analysts anticipated a loss of $0.05 per share for Q1 2022, compared to earnings of $0.02 in the prior year. Estimated revenue was projected at $171.25 million, representing a 20% decline from the previous year's $214.09 million. BlackBerry had missed earnings estimates in the past eight quarters and faced similar risks going forward. The anticipated revenue decrease would primarily stem from the License and Other segment. Investors were watching for improved performance in the Enterprise Software Services segment, which had underperformed for several consecutive quarters. Additionally, the market sought to validate whether the stock's strong performance could be justified by fundamental business improvements. Overall, BB received a Moderate Sell rating with an average price target of C$11.20, implying 29% downside potential from then-current levels.
